title: Ethics and Conflicts of Interest - FEDERAL RESERVE BANK of NEW YORK
gist: This is a NY Fed governance page on employee ethics and conflict-of-interest rules, not a Liberty Street analytical post. No market analysis, but it documents notable trading/ownership restrictions: Markets Group and FOMC-information staff may not own primary dealers, and a Personal Trading Compliance Program requires pre-clearance of trades.

insights.claim: NY Fed Markets Group staff and those with regular access to Class I FOMC information are barred from owning or controlling investments in any primary dealer (or its parent), with the restriction extending to spouses and minor children.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Context for how the Fed firewalls market-sensitive staff after the 2021 trading scandal; relevant to credibility of Fed policy independence.
insights.quote: staff members in the Markets Group and those with regular and ongoing access to Class I Federal Open Market Committee information may not own or control investments in a primary dealer

insights.claim: Designated NY Fed employees must pre-clear every securities transaction with the Ethics Office under a Personal Trading Compliance Program and route covered accounts through a Designated Broker.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Documents the post-scandal trading-control regime; no direct market signal but bears on Fed governance reputation.
